title: Projects
section: Leptospira Diversity and Abundance in Fishers
text:
Leptospira Diversity and Abundance in Fishers
Investigating the number of Fishers infected with leptospira as well as the diversity of the leptospira.

title: Projects
section: Spatial and Diet Impacts on the Microbiota of New England Wild Canids
text:
Spatial and Diet Impacts on the Microbiota of New England Wild Canids
Determining if diet composition alters the microbiome of wild caninds or if spatial differences cause a greater difference.

title: Unessay
text:
I am Zeb Antonioli. I am originally from Montana and am currently studying Microbiology at the University of New Hampshire. I originally got the photography bug in 2018 on a trip to New Zealand. From there, I was hooked. I have done multiple generas of photography; however, my favorites are astrophotography and wildlife photography. This unessay assignment is meant to marry my academic interests with my passion of wildlife photography. Over the course of the past 6 years, I have had the privilege of photography many different animals, and here I will present relevant papers and research that has been conducted specifically on the microbiome or portion of the microbiome of various species I have photographed. I will accompany this information with images I have taken along with a personal artist statement. I hope to return to my wildlife photography work as it has been put on the backburner due to my academics. Ideally, I will be able to persue both once I graduate.
Weathered — Docked on the side of the Chobe, this lone bull slowly walked past our boat. I went on the bow and leaned as far as I could to get the head on shot. He came about 20 meters from me and just kept moving down the river indifferent to our presence. This portrait was a shot that I had envisioned for months and was so happy when I had the chance to capture it.
Timeless — .
Playa Selfie
Million Miles per Hour

title: Unessay
text: My current goals are to obtain my PhD in Microbiology with a primary focus on wildlife conservation, ecology, and disease. My primary focuses are on genetic disease, impacts on the normal microbiome, and emerging diseases within various wildlife species. I hope to work with African wildlife once I have obtained my PhD. This unessay assignment is meant to marry my academic interests with my passion of wildlife photography. Over the course of the past 6 years, I have had the privilege of photography many different animals and here I will present relevant papers and research that has been conducted specifically on the microbiome or portion of the microbiome of various animal species. I will accompany this information with images I have taken along with a personal artist statement.

title: About Me
text:
Undergraduate
I received my B.A. from Carroll College Helena MT in 2022. I double majored in Biochemistry and Molecular Biology (BMB) as well as Biology.
Graduate
I am currently a Microbiology PhD Graduate Student at the University of New Hampshire. I am within the College of Life Sciences (COLSA) and Agriculture and the department of Molecular, Cellular, and Biomedical Sciences (MCBS). I am in my second year of the program. I am also a Graduate Student Teaching Assistant for General Microbiology.

title: About Me
text:
Undergraduate Research
I worked on monitoring for West Nile Virus presence in mosquito pools across the state of MT. This included RNA extractions and qPCR. I also performed a comparative gene expression experiment to determine if the expression of a pigmentation gene differed across populations of mosquitoes in MT.
Molecular Diagnostics
I worked as a molecular diagnostic laboratory technician for the MTVDL. I worked with various infectious agents of high agricultural impact such as High Pathogenic Avian Influenza. This involved high throughput extractions and qPCR as well as customer service with clients and delivering results in a timely manner.
Graduate Research
I have many projects occuring as of now which can be found under my Projects tab.

title: African Elephants
text:
Introduction
Loxodonta africanan is arguably one of the most charismatic mega fauna species alive. Elephants have always captivated me since I was young. From the sheer size to the complex social structures, they remind me that we are not the only intelligent species on this earth. In 2023 I went to both Botswana and Kenya. In Chobe National Park near Kasane Botswana, the elephant populations are more tolerant of humans when compared to those of South Africa and eastern Africa. The lack of hunting and human wildlife conflict has allowed for these individuals to be more relaxed around people. This is not to say they are tame as I found out when one of the other boats was charged and tusked by a rouge male. I was informed this one of only a handful of instances of elephants behaving aggressively in this region. My experiences thankfully were not of the terrifying kind. One moment I was unable to photograph was our boat drifting close to an island in the channel of the river. On this island there was a small herd feeding with the matriarch and her calf near the water. I cannot say for certain, but we came within only mere meters of the matriarch. Nothing can describe watching as she flared her ears out and her calf hid behind her. I still get giddy thinking about that moment. Kenya was slightly less magical; however, I cannot understate how incredible being able to create artwork of these animals is.






Figure 2: Flooded Lunch — While exploring the Chobe, there were many instances where we came across spread family groups that had crossed the river to access food as can be seen by the high water mark on the mother’s side. This cow calf pair were busy munching away at the waterlogged grass. I enjoy being able to sit and enjoy simple moments like foraging.

title: African Elephants
section: A metagenomic survey of the fecal microbiome of the African savanna elephant (Loxodonta africana). Preez et al., 2024
text:
A metagenomic survey of the fecal microbiome of the African savanna elephant (Loxodonta africana). Preez et al., 2024
The Preez et al., paper investigated the fecal microbiome of semi-captive elephants from South Africa ranging in both age and sex. The DNA from each indivudual elephant’s feces was extracted and 16S rRNA gene and ITS2 were used for PCR to amplify the bacterial/archeal and fungal components of the fecal microbiome respectively. Sequencing and subseqeunt analysis of these amplicons allowed the researchers to establish the relative abundance of each major taxa within the fecal microbiome. Subseqeunt metagenomic shotgun sequening was performed to determine what functional genes were present that could be attributed to nutrient metabolism and aquasition. From the amplicon analysis, microbial communiyt profiles were obtained providing a baseline characterization of savanna elephant microbiome components. This in of itself is a novel analysis and characterization for the bacterial, archeal, and fungal communiites. The metagenomic analysis showed that the majority of the cataboic pathway genes were associated with carbohydrate degradation. This result is novel yet substantiated by previous research of other hind gut fermenters such as horses and rhinos. Overall, the research provided baseline fecal microbiome profiles as well as an in depth exploration of the various carbohydrate catabolic genes present.






Figure 3: Just like mom — There is a special place in my heart each time we encountered young calves within a group. While the older individuals paid us no mind, the calves always stuck close to mom. It creates wonderful intimate moments. The overexposed vegitation really allows the details both on the cow and the calf to stand out making the image pop.









Figure 4: Beach Solitude — Docked on the side of the Chobe, this lone bull slowly walked past our boat. I went on the bow and leaned as far as I could to get the head on shot. He came about 20 meters from me and just kept moving down the river indifferent to our presence.









Figure 5: Motion — Focusing on part of the whole offers a different perspective. The mud being propelled off of the foot and the swing of the trunk are not a typical image of an elephant yet unmistakably is part of the whole.
